response: convert the art collection tax by applying the 5% discount to the 20% rate, resulting in an effective 19% rate on the $2 million value, which equals $380,000. convert the real estate tax calculation for the initial $5 million valuation by applying 10% to the first $1 million, 15% to the next $2 million, and 20% to the remaining $2 million, totaling $1,100,000. convert the securities tax by subtracting the $100,000 charitable deduction from the $3 million value to get a taxable base of $2.9 million, then applying the 15% rate for a liability of $435,000. convert the sum of these three components to find the initial total estate tax liability of $1,915,000. convert the real estate valuation upward by 10% to reflect the correction, making the new total value $5.5 million. convert the new real estate tax by applying 10% to the first $1 million, 15% to the next $2 million, and 20% to the remaining $2.5 million, which results in a new liability of $1,200,000. convert the final total estate tax liability by adding the unchanged art and securities taxes to the corrected real estate tax, yielding a new total of $2,015,000.
